previously_flagged_now_resolved: 08_hw_conditions: 'Very large population' upgraded to 'Infinitely large population (no genetic drift)' — exactly the phrasing I suggested, and the narration now also makes the 1:1 mapping to the five mechanisms in section 11 explicit. Clean fix.

11_mechanisms: assortative mating (by phenotype) and inbreeding (by relatedness) are now distinguished as 'related but not the same thing' in both the slide hint and the narration. Resolved exactly as recommended.

12_drift_examples: cheetah bottleneck timing claim was dropped — the narration now just says 'when a population crashes, like the cheetah bottleneck, the survivors carry only a sliver of the original variation,' which is correct without overstating the literature. Resolved.

12_drift_examples: 'polydactyly' upgraded to 'Ellis-van Creveld syndrome, which includes polydactyly along with other traits, ... traced to a single founder couple.' Exactly the fix I recommended. Resolved.

04_darwin_postulates: although I flagged the Mayr reformulation framing as only a note (not requiring change), the revisor also tightened postulate 2 from 'Variation is heritable' to 'Some variation is heritable (has a genetic component)' which is a noticeable improvement in precision and avoids the implication that all variation is heritable.
06_selection_types and 14_homology_trap: my round-1 notes here did not require changes, and none of the wording introduced new factual problems.
previously_flagged_still_open: 04_darwin_postulates: the 'four postulates' framing is still presented as 'Darwin's argument is just four steps' without acknowledging this is the modern Mayr/Endler didactic reconstruction rather than Darwin's own enumeration. I flagged this as a 'note' severity in round 1 and explicitly said it was defensible for AP — the revisor's choice to keep it as-is is consistent with my round-1 recommendation. Listing here for completeness but I do not consider this an unresolved issue.
overall_assessment: The revision addresses every round-1 factual concern I raised that warranted a change. The four 'minor'-severity items — 'infinitely large population' phrasing, the assortative-vs-inbreeding distinction, the cheetah-bottleneck-timing softening, and the Ellis-van Creveld upgrade from bare 'polydactyly' — are all resolved cleanly and in the exact spirit I recommended. The Hardy-Weinberg equations, the worked practice problem (q² = 0.16 → q = 0.4 → p = 0.6), the fitness definition (now sharpened with the 'top reproducer = 1, others are a fraction' elaboration), the selection-type examples, the bottleneck/founder distinction, the homologous/analogous distinction, and the six lines of evidence all remain factually correct. The revision also strengthens the script in ways beyond my round-1 asks — the explicit 'natural selection acts on phenotypes, not directly on genotypes' callout, the 'mutations pre-exist the selective pressure' (anti-Lamarckism) framing on both peppered moths and antibiotic resistance, the 'drift is non-adaptive' line, and the 'evolution has no goal / no progress ladder' callout are all real upgrades that close common AP misconceptions. The only round-1 note left unaddressed is the framing of Darwin's 'four postulates' as Darwin's own enumeration rather than the modern Mayr reformulation, which I explicitly accepted as defensible for AP. I found no new factual errors in the revised outline. Verdict: pass.
